seo.title: CGWire {'|'} Open Metrics
seo.description: Open company metrics for CGWire: revenue, customers, funding and more.

hero.title: Our numbers, in full transparency
hero.lead: We are part of the Open Company movement. Transparency helps build trust with our clients, our team and our community, so this page shares the main metrics of our company, focused mainly on the accounting side.

revenue.title: Our revenue, what we bill
revenue.text: The money we billed through issued invoices, before taxes.

mrr.title: Our monthly recurring revenue
mrr.text: We mainly sell yearly subscriptions. This chart spreads them over 12 months to show the recurring revenue we can count on each month, regardless of when a subscription starts.

customers.title: How many use Kitsu
customers.activeText: Studios with a running subscription. Open source users are not counted here.
customers.selfhostedText: Teams running Kitsu on their own servers, independently from our cloud offering.

team.title: Our Salaries
team.text: We pay salaries at market level, as the French "net" amount before taxes. Part-time is welcome: the fraction next to a role shows how much of a full week it covers.

captable.title: How ownership is split between shareholders

tools.title: Our tools
tools.text: The products and services we run on.

servers.title: Our servers
servers.text: Everything runs on the OVH public cloud, billed monthly and scaling with what we use. Teams that self-host run on their own servers, not on this bill.

item1.title: To stay aligned with our mission
item1.text: Sharing our numbers keeps us honest about the decisions we make and the company we want to build.

item2.title: To challenge ourselves
item2.text: Public metrics are a strong incentive to keep improving and to hold ourselves accountable.

item3.title: To show it is possible
item3.text: A small, self-funded software company can be sustainable and open about how it works.

carbon.title: Discover our 2024 carbon report
carbon.text: Transparency is not only about money. We also measure and share the environmental footprint of our activity. The 2024 report breaks down our main emission sources, where we can still improve, and the concrete actions we are taking to reduce our footprint.
